NKJV.1: A Psalm. A Song at the dedication of the house of David. I will extol You, O LORD, for You have lifted me up, And have not let my foes rejoice over me.
NKJV.2: O LORD my God, I cried out to You, And You healed me.
NKJV.3: O LORD, You brought my soul up from the grave; You have kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.
NKJV.4: Sing praise to the LORD, you saints of His, And give thanks at the remembrance of His holy name.
NKJV.5: For His anger is but for a moment, His favor is for life; Weeping may endure for a night, But joy comes in the morning.
NKJV.6: Now in my prosperity I said, “I shall never be moved.”
NKJV.7: LORD, by Your favor You have made my mountain stand strong; You hid Your face, and I was troubled.
NKJV.8: I cried out to You, O LORD; And to the LORD I made supplication:
NKJV.9: “What profit is there in my blood, When I go down to the pit? Will the dust praise You? Will it declare Your truth?
NKJV.10: Hear, O LORD, and have mercy on me; LORD, be my helper!”
NKJV.11: You have turned for me my mourning into dancing; You have put off my sackcloth and clothed me with gladness,
NKJV.12: To the end that my glory may sing praise to You and not be silent. O LORD my God, I will give thanks to You forever.

KJV.8: I cried<S>7121</S> to thee, O LORD<S>3068</S>; and unto the LORD<S>3068</S> I made supplication<S>2603</S>.

ESV.1: I will extol you, O LORD, for you have drawn me up and have not let my foes rejoice over me.
ESV.2: O LORD my God, I cried to you for help, and you have healed me.
ESV.3: O LORD, you have brought up my soul from Sheol; you restored me to life from among those who go down to the pit.
ESV.4: Sing praises to the LORD, O you his saints, and give thanks to his holy name.
ESV.5: For his anger is but for a moment, and his favor is for a lifetime.  Weeping may tarry for the night, but joy comes with the morning.
ESV.6: As for me, I said in my prosperity, “I shall never be moved.”
ESV.7: By your favor, O LORD, you made my mountain stand strong; you hid your face; I was dismayed.
ESV.8: To you, O LORD, I cry, and to the Lord I plead for mercy:
ESV.9: “What profit is there in my death,  if I go down to the pit?  Will the dust praise you? Will it tell of your faithfulness?
ESV.10: Hear, O LORD, and be merciful to me! O LORD, be my helper!”
ESV.11: You have turned for me my mourning into dancing; you have loosed my sackcloth and clothed me with gladness,
ESV.12: that my glory may sing your praise and not be silent. O LORD my God, I will give thanks to you forever!

NASB.1: A Psalm; a Song at the Dedication of the House. [A Psalm] of David. I will extol You, O LORD, for You have lifted me up, And have not let my enemies rejoice over me.
NASB.2: O LORD my God, I cried to You for help, and You healed me.
NASB.3: O LORD, You have brought up my soul from Sheol; You have kept me alive, that I would not go down to the pit.
NASB.4: Sing praise to the LORD, you His godly ones, And give thanks to His holy name.
NASB.5: For His anger is but for a moment, His favor is for a lifetime; Weeping may last for the night, But a shout of joy [comes] in the morning.
NASB.6: Now as for me, I said in my prosperity, 'I will never be moved.'
NASB.7: O LORD, by Your favor You have made my mountain to stand strong; You hid Your face, I was dismayed.
NASB.8: To You, O LORD, I called, And to the Lord I made supplication:
NASB.9: 'What profit is there in my blood, if I go down to the pit? Will the dust praise You? Will it declare Your faithfulness?
NASB.10: 'Hear, O LORD, and be gracious to me; O LORD, be my helper.'
NASB.11: You have turned for me my mourning into dancing; You have loosed my sackcloth and girded me with gladness,
NASB.12: That [my] soul may sing praise to You and not be silent. O LORD my God, I will give thanks to You forever.

NLT.1:
I will exalt you, LORD, for you rescued me.
You refused to let my enemies triumph over me.
NLT.2:
O LORD my God, I cried to you for help,
and you restored my health.
NLT.3:
You brought me up from the grave, O LORD.
You kept me from falling into the pit of death.
NLT.4:
Sing to the LORD, all you godly ones!
Praise his holy name.
NLT.5:
For his anger lasts only a moment,
but his favor lasts a lifetime!
Weeping may last through the night,
but joy comes with the morning.
NLT.6:
When I was prosperous, I said,
“Nothing can stop me now!”
NLT.7:
Your favor, O LORD, made me as secure as a mountain.
Then you turned away from me, and I was shattered.
NLT.8:
I cried out to you, O LORD.
I begged the Lord for mercy, saying,
NLT.9:
“What will you gain if I die,
if I sink into the grave?
Can my dust praise you?
Can it tell of your faithfulness?
NLT.10:
Hear me, LORD, and have mercy on me.
Help me, O LORD.”
NLT.11:
You have turned my mourning into joyful dancing.
You have taken away my clothes of mourning and clothed me with joy,
NLT.12:
that I might sing praises to you and not be silent.
O LORD my God, I will give you thanks forever!

WEB.1: A Psalm. A Song for the Dedication of the Temple. By David. I will extol you, Yahweh, for you have raised me up, and have not made my foes to rejoice over me.
WEB.2: Yahweh my God, I cried to you, and you have healed me.
WEB.3: Yahweh, you have brought up my soul from Sheol.  You have kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.
WEB.4: Sing praise to Yahweh, you saints of his. Give thanks to his holy name.
WEB.5: For his anger is but for a moment. His favor is for a lifetime. Weeping may stay for the night, but joy comes in the morning.
WEB.6: As for me, I said in my prosperity, “I shall never be moved.”
WEB.7: You, Yahweh, when you favored me, made my mountain stand strong; but when you hid your face, I was troubled.
WEB.8: I cried to you, Yahweh. I made supplication to the Lord:
WEB.9: “What profit is there in my destruction, if I go down to the pit? Shall the dust praise you? Shall it declare your truth?
WEB.10: Hear, Yahweh, and have mercy on me. Yahweh, be my helper.”
WEB.11: You have turned my mourning into dancing for me. You have removed my sackcloth, and clothed me with gladness,
WEB.12: To the end that my heart may sing praise to you, and not be silent. Yahweh my God, I will give thanks to you forever!

CSB17.1:
A psalm; a dedication song for the house. Of David. [1] I will exalt you, LORD,
because you have lifted me up ⓑ
and have not allowed my enemies
to triumph over me. ⓒ
CSB17.2:
LORD my God,
I cried to you for help, and you healed me. ⓓ
CSB17.3:
LORD, you brought me up from Sheol; ⓔ
you spared me from among those
going down [1] to the Pit. ⓕ
CSB17.4:
Sing to the LORD, you his faithful ones,
and praise his holy name. ⓖ
CSB17.5:
For his anger lasts only a moment,
but his favor, a lifetime.
Weeping may stay overnight,
but there is joy in the morning. ⓗ
CSB17.6:
When I was secure, I said,
“I will never be shaken.” ⓘ
CSB17.7:
LORD, when you showed your favor,
you made me stand like a strong mountain; ⓙ
when you hid your face, I was terrified. ⓚ
CSB17.8:
LORD, I called to you;
I sought favor from my Lord: ⓛ
CSB17.9:
“What gain is there in my death,
if I go down to the Pit?
Will the dust praise you?
Will it proclaim your truth? ⓜ
CSB17.10:
LORD, listen and be gracious to me;
LORD, be my helper.” ⓝ
CSB17.11:
You turned my lament into dancing;
you removed my sackcloth
and clothed me with gladness, ⓐ
CSB17.12:
so that I can sing to you and not be silent.
LORD my God, I will praise you forever. ⓑ

AMP.1:
A Psalm; a Song at the Dedication of the House (Temple). A Psalm of David. I will extol and praise You, O LORD, for You have lifted me up,
And have not let my enemies rejoice over me.
AMP.2:
O LORD my God,
I cried to You for help, and You have healed me.
AMP.3:
O LORD, You have brought my life up from Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead);
You have kept me alive, so that I would not go down to the pit (grave).
AMP.4:
Sing to the LORD, O you His godly ones,
And give thanks at the mention of His holy name.
AMP.5:
For His anger is but for a moment,
His favor is for a lifetime.
Weeping may endure for a night,
But a shout of joy comes in the morning.
AMP.6:
As for me, in my prosperity I said,
"I shall never be moved."
AMP.7:
By Your favor and grace, O LORD, you have made my mountain stand strong;
You hid Your face, and I was horrified.
AMP.8:
I called to You, O LORD,
And to the Lord I made supplication (specific request).
AMP.9:
"What profit is there in my blood (death), if I go down to the pit (grave)?
Will the dust praise You? Will it declare Your faithfulness [to man]?
AMP.10:
"Hear, O LORD, be gracious and show favor to me;
O LORD, be my helper."
AMP.11:
You have turned my mourning into dancing for me;
You have taken off my sackcloth and clothed me with joy,
AMP.12:
That my soul may sing praise to You and not be silent.
O LORD my God, I will give thanks to You forever.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
I give you all the credit, God—
you got me out of that mess,
you didn't let my foes gloat.
MSG.2:
God, my God, I yelled for help
and you put me together.
MSG.3:
God, you pulled me out of the grave,
gave me another chance at life
when I was down-and-out.
MSG.4:
All you saints! Sing your hearts out to God!
Thank him to his face!
MSG.5:
He gets angry once in a while, but across
a lifetime there is only love.
The nights of crying your eyes out
give way to days of laughter.
MSG.6:
When things were going great
I crowed, "I've got it made.
MSG.7:
I'm God's favorite.
He made me king of the mountain."
Then you looked the other way
and I fell to pieces.
MSG.8:
I called out to you, God;
I laid my case before you:
MSG.9:
"Can you sell me for a profit when I'm dead?
auction me off at a cemetery yard sale?
When I'm 'dust to dust' my songs
and stories of you won't sell.
MSG.10:
So listen! and be kind!
Help me out of this!"
MSG.11:
You did it: you changed wild lament
into whirling dance;
You ripped off my black mourning band
and decked me with wildflowers.
MSG.12:
I'm about to burst with song;
I can't keep quiet about you.
God, my God,
I can't thank you enough.

NIV.1:
Psalm 30
A psalm. A song. For the dedication of the temple. Of David.
I will exalt you, O Lord,
for you lifted me out of the depths
and did not let my enemies gloat over me.
NIV.2:
O Lord my God, I called to you for help
and you healed me.
NIV.3:
O Lord, you brought me up from the grave;
you spared me from going down into the pit.
NIV.4:
Sing to the Lord, you saints of his;
praise his holy name.
NIV.5:
For his anger lasts only a moment,
but his favor lasts a lifetime;
weeping may remain for a night,
but rejoicing comes in the morning.
NIV.6:
When I felt secure, I said,
“I will never be shaken.”
NIV.7:
O Lord, when you favored me,
you made my mountain stand firm;
but when you hid your face,
I was dismayed.
NIV.8:
To you, O Lord, I called;
to the Lord I cried for mercy:
NIV.9:
“What gain is there in my destruction,
in my going down into the pit?
Will the dust praise you?
Will it proclaim your faithfulness?
NIV.10:
Hear, O Lord, and be merciful to me;
O Lord, be my help.”
NIV.11:
You turned my wailing into dancing;
you removed my sackcloth and clothed me with joy,
NIV.12:
that my heart may sing to you and not be silent.
O Lord my God, I will give you thanks forever.

NRSVCE.1:
I will extol you, O LORD, for you have drawn me up,
and did not let my foes rejoice over me.
NRSVCE.2:
O LORD my God, I cried to you for help,
and you have healed me.
NRSVCE.3:
O LORD, you brought up my soul from Sheol,
restored me to life from among those gone down to the Pit.
NRSVCE.4:
Sing praises to the LORD, O you his faithful ones,
and give thanks to his holy name.
NRSVCE.5:
For his anger is but for a moment;
his favor is for a lifetime.
Weeping may linger for the night,
but joy comes with the morning.
NRSVCE.6:
As for me, I said in my prosperity,
“I shall never be moved.”
NRSVCE.7:
By your favor, O LORD,
you had established me as a strong mountain;
you hid your face;
I was dismayed.
NRSVCE.8:
To you, O LORD, I cried,
and to the LORD I made supplication:
NRSVCE.9:
“What profit is there in my death,
if I go down to the Pit?
Will the dust praise you?
Will it tell of your faithfulness?
NRSVCE.10:
Hear, O LORD, and be gracious to me!
O LORD, be my helper!”
NRSVCE.11:
You have turned my mourning into dancing;
you have taken off my sackcloth
and clothed me with joy,
NRSVCE.12:
so that my soul may praise you and not be silent.
O LORD my God, I will give thanks to you forever.

NET.1: I will praise you, O LORD, for you lifted me up, and did not allow my enemies to gloat over me.
NET.2: O LORD my God, I cried out to you and you healed me.
NET.3: O LORD, you pulled me up from Sheol; you rescued me from among those descending into the grave.
NET.4: Sing to the LORD, you faithful followers of his; give thanks to his holy name.
NET.5: For his anger lasts only a brief moment, and his good favor restores one’s life. One may experience sorrow during the night, but joy arrives in the morning.
NET.6: In my self-confidence I said, “I will never be upended.”
NET.7: O LORD, in your good favor you made me secure. Then you rejected me and I was terrified.
NET.8: To you, O LORD, I cried out; I begged the Lord for mercy:
NET.9: “What profit is there in taking my life, in my descending into the Pit? Can the dust of the grave praise you? Can it declare your loyalty?
NET.10: Hear, O LORD, and have mercy on me! O LORD, deliver me!”
NET.11: Then you turned my lament into dancing; you removed my sackcloth and covered me with joy.
NET.12: So now my heart will sing to you and not be silent; O LORD my God, I will always give thanks to you.
